Palpable purpura is a hallmark sign of Henoch-Schönlein purpura (HSP), mixed cryoglobulinemia, giant cell arteritis, and drug-induced vasculitis. However, it specifically differentiates itself when considering the pathogenesis – HSP being due to IgA deposits versus immune complex mediated mechanisms in others. Therefore,
\boxed{C} Based on the detailed review of the literature, palpable purpura is characteristic of Henoch-Schönlein purpura, mixed cryoglobulinemia, and drug-induced vasculitis, each having specific contributing factors like IgA deposition and immune complex formation respectively.
